title: Logging arguments should be passed to the correct parameter
description: Logging arguments should be passed to the correct parameterAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityCode SmellloggingWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoMost logging frameworks have methods that take a log level, an event ID or an exception as a separate input next to the log format and its arguments. There is a high chance that if the log level, the event ID or the exception are passed as the arguments to the message format, it was a mistake. This rule is going to raise in that scenario.The rule covers the following logging frameworks:Nuget package -Castle.CoreNuget package -SerilogNuget package -NLogNuget package -Microsoft.Extensions.LoggingAvailable In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab rep
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.reasoning: Exceptions and LogLevels should be passed as dedicated parameters, not as message template arguments
